mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing present values of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during rate cuts show no statistically significant positive mean (p > 0.05) over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day equity returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, with a mean return significantly below zero (t-test p < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 260 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, contradicting the tightening premium hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with a cumulative alpha of zero over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand growth, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates fail to correlate with 60-day equity returns, producing a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly decline in industrial production signals an imminent supply-side contraction and inventory buildup, which suppresses near-term earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops greater than 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter are followed by positive 5-day equity returns in over 70 percent of cases.
